title: erigeron acris subsp. botschantzevii
intro: Erigeron acris subsp. botschantzevii, also known as the Botschantzev's daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds and gastrointestinal problems. The health benefits of Erigeron acris subsp. botschantzevii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
